chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2640 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلنَّهْدِيِّ قَالَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تُحْرِمُ فِي اَلْعِمَامَةِ وَ لَهَا عَلَمٌ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2640 - It was narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Nahdi, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked, while I was present, about a woman entering Ihram wearing a turban (imamah) with an emblem (alam).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
